note: Historical record — NOT current status. Reconstructed from dated authority notices, government reports and news. Confidence flagged per event.

events.notes: High levels of blue-green algae; GMW warned to avoid Lake Mulwala, WaterNSW cautioned the Murray River downstream. Start = article publication date (13 Feb 2020, warning newly issued). No lift date found; end_date null.

Red alert blue-green algae warning issued for the whole of Lake Hume; Border Mail (pub 25 Feb 2020) states the alert was issued 'on Monday' = 24 Feb 2020. No lift date located; end_date null. No cell count stated. WaterNSW is the issuing authority; corroborated by dated news. Lake Hume straddles VIC/NSW border and is included per instructions.

High levels first detected July 2020 (month only, used 1st); warning removed October 2021 after 467 days - described as the longest on record for the lake. End date approximated to Alexandra Newspapers publication date 28 Oct 2021. Corroborated by Shepparton News 21 Dec 2020 ('warning persists').

GWMWater warning to avoid direct contact after testing detected blue-green algae above safe recreation levels. start_date is the article publication date (16 Nov 2020) announcing the warning; exact issue date not separately stated. A GWMWater media release later declared the outbreak 'over' (lift date not confirmed), so end_date left null.
Avoid-contact warning reported by Bendigo Advertiser, first published 25 Jan 2021; exact issue date not stated so start approximated to article date. End/removal date not found.

events.region: Hume Weir (whole storage), Upper Murray / VIC-NSW border
events.notes: WaterNSW issued a red alert ('high alert') for the whole of Hume Weir on Christmas Eve, 24 Dec 2021 (Border Mail pub 25 Dec 2021). No lift date located (would have lifted early 2022); end_date null. No cell count stated.

events.authority: Department of Energy, Environment and Climate Action (Victoria)
events.notes: Part of the 2022 Gippsland Lakes Nodularia bloom. Warning for Lake Wellington was reported lifted by 14 April 2022 as counts fell; exact issue date not stated so start set to 1 March 2022 (month approximate). Low confidence.

events.authority: Department of Energy, Environment and Climate Action (Victoria)
events.notes: Toxic Nodularia spumigena bloom. High-level recreational alert plus Do Not Eat Seafood advisory across all Gippsland Lakes. Detected/warned ~14 April 2022 (Easter), high through 17 May 2022 update; declined to low level by 10 June 2022 update (seafood advisory continued). end_date left null as recreational warning wound down gradually June 2022.

Nodularia spumigena bloom across Gippsland Lakes (Lake Victoria, Lake King, Loch Sport, Bunga Arm, etc.), spreading along Ninety Mile Beach. 'Do Not Eat Seafood' advisory plus gut-and-gill fish advisory; high toxin levels detected in seafood. water.vic.gov.au update last updated 17 May 2022; DHHS health warning re-issued 27 May 2022. This is a DHHS/health-stream event (not a water-corporation storage), included here because confirmed; likely overlaps another source stream for dedup. No numeric cell count.

events.region: Hume Dam, Upper Murray / VIC-NSW border
events.notes: Red alert issued Tuesday 10 Oct 2022 (Border Mail 7939895, surface scums widespread across the storage) and downgraded/lifted 14 Nov 2022 per the WaterNSW 'red alert warning lifted – Hume Dam' page (fetched, pub 14 Nov 2022) and Border Mail 7980762 (pub 14 Nov 2022, 'red alert has been downgraded'). No cell count stated. WaterNSW authority page confirms the lift; issue date from dated news.
GMW warning after high levels detected; Bendigo Advertiser first published 11 Jan 2023. Removal date not found.
Warning issued 20 Jan 2023 advising avoid direct contact; boating/sightseeing still permitted. No numeric cell count published. Lift date not stated.
Warning for Hepburns Lagoon reported alongside Tullaroop Reservoir; Bendigo Advertiser first published 26 Jan 2023. Start approximated to article date. No removal date found.

Warning for Tullaroop Reservoir reported alongside Hepburns Lagoon; Bendigo Advertiser first published 26 Jan 2023. Exact issue date not stated, start approximated to article date. No removal date found.

High levels, avoid contact. Article (28 Jan 2023) notes two additional central-Vic storages detected the same algae earlier that week (part of the Jan 2023 Eppalock/Tullaroop/Hepburns outbreak). Start = publication date; no lift date found.
GMW customer letter dated 21 Feb 2023 for high/potentially toxic blue-green algae at Racecourse Lake and Kangaroo Lake (Torrumbarry Irrigation Area). Removal date not found.
Same GMW customer letter (21 Feb 2023) covering Racecourse Lake with Kangaroo Lake. Removal date not found.

Sunbury Life report. Council last tested water in March 2023 and blue-green algae was detected, with warning signs erected. Only the month (March) is known for detection, so start_date set to 2023-03-01 (1st of month, approximate). Re-test around 15 May 2023 showed algae no longer present (end_date). No severity classification or cell counts given.
GWMWater warning that algae exceeded safe recreation levels; avoid direct water contact. start_date = article date 17 May 2023. No numeric cell count. Lift date not stated.
Warning issued December 2023, removed 24 Jan 2024 after 47 days (Shepparton News). Start back-calculated (~8 Dec 2023) from the stated 47-day duration - approximate.
GMW customer letter dated 10 Jan 2024; high levels in Broken River downstream of Lake Nillahcootie (to Casey Weir, incl. Lake Benalla). Removal date not found.

Council notice dated Tuesday 16 January 2024: monitoring 'detected high levels of blue-green algae' at Lake Bartlett, Tatura. A later council 'levels decline' page marks the end but its date was not captured (WebFetch rate-limited). No cells/mL figure.
GMW media release: warning issued 27 Feb 2024, remained in place 127 days. End date (~3 July 2024) computed from 127-day duration; approximate to within a day.

Council media release dated 28 February 2024: lab analysis found 'an unacceptable level of blue green algae species'; advised avoiding all direct contact until further advice. No lift date or cells/mL figure on this page.
Council warning issued Thursday 14 March 2024 after detecting high levels of blue-green algae at Kialla Lakes; avoid direct contact. No cell count stated.

Council page published 18 March 2024: 'Regular testing has detected levels of blue-green algae above levels considered safe for recreation use' at Lake Wallace. No lift date on this page. Distinct from the 2025 event.
Council media release dated 27 March 2024: samples confirmed 'high levels of the toxic algae' at Green Lake near Horsham. No lift date stated on this page. No cells/mL figure given.
GMW posted the warning on 22 April 2024 (Shepparton News). Warning removed and reported in article published 18 Jul 2024 but exact removal date not stated - end date left null.
Warning issued ~1 May 2024 per Ararat Rural City Council; Parks Victoria cautioned avoid-contact. Removal date not found.

A DEECA 'Gippsland Lakes Algae Update - 31 May 2024' (and a '15 March 2024' update) appear in water.vic.gov.au search results, indicating a 2024 Gippsland Lakes algae season, but BOTH PDFs returned HTTP 404 when fetched, so lakes/levels/cell counts could not be confirmed. Date is the report title date, not a verified bloom start. Low confidence - flagged for re-verification.

Bendigo Advertiser article published 8 October 2024 (updated 9 Oct): BGA bloom at Lake Neangar verified by City of Greater Bendigo environmental health coordinator; residents advised to avoid contact. No cells/mL figure. Council did not have a separate dated release located.
GMW warning issued 9 Dec 2024, remained in place 60 days, lifted 11 Feb 2025 (dates attributed to Goulburn-Murray Water). No cell count.
Warning issued 12 Dec 2024 (Greater Shepparton City Council); removed by 6 Jan 2025 (removal announcement date) — exact removal date not stated.

Hume City Council media release. Blue-green algae bloom confirmed at Spavin Lake, Sunbury on 7 January 2025; warning to avoid water contact. Follow-up inspection 7 February 2025 found no visible signs of algae, but warning not formally lifted ('conditions can change rapidly'). end_date 2025-02-07 is the 'no visible signs' date, used as best estimate of clearance. Related council Instagram/Facebook 'algae update' posts appear from ~Dec 2024, suggesting the bloom may have begun late Dec 2024. No cell counts stated.
Warning (Hepburns Lagoon and Hepburns Race) issued 9 Jan 2025, removed 4 Sep 2025 after 238 days per GMW combined removal notice. Issue date also confirmed by GMW Hepburns notice page.

Warning issued 14 Feb 2025, removed 4 Sep 2025 after 202 days per GMW combined removal notice. Issue date also confirmed by GMW Cairn Curran in-place notice (14 Feb 2025) and Country News 16 Feb 2025.
Warning issued 27 Feb 2025, removed 4 Sep 2025 after 189 days per GMW combined removal notice. (GMW Tullaroop notice page states 28 Feb 2025 - one-day discrepancy in sources.)
GMW media release dated 5 Mar 2025, high levels detected. Removal date not confirmed (a later 'Laanecoorie Reservoir and Tullaroop Creek warning removed' report exists but was not fetched).

Warning lifted per West Wimmera Shire Council page dated 12 June 2025 ('Recent testing indicates the water is safe'). Issue date ~19 March 2025 taken from Wimmera Mallee News article slug/date (wimmeramalleenews.com.au/blue-green-algae-in-lake-wallace-2025-03-19); issue-date confidence medium, lift-date confidence high. Council 2025 warning page existed but could not be fetched (rate-limited).
GMW notice dated 20 March 2025: high levels at Lake Boga and Lake Charm, avoid contact. No lift date found.
Warning issued 20 Mar 2025 (jointly with Lake Boga), removed 30 Apr 2025 after 41 days per GMW notice.
Lake closed after Dolichospermum at biovolume 8.653 mm3/L (biovolume, not cells/mL, so cell count null); council notice published 21 Mar 2025. Removal date not found.

Regional masthead (The Weekly Advertiser, 29 April 2025) lists Green Lake among Wimmera lakes with an active BGA warning ('above levels considered safe for recreation use') alongside Lake Lonsdale and Lake Wallace. Exact issue date not stated; start_date set to 1 April 2025 as a month-only placeholder (bloom active as of 29 Apr 2025). Distinct from the March 2024 Green Lake event.

Council media release published 4 Apr 2025: avoid swimming/direct contact with algae-affected water at Lake Victoria, Maryborough. This is the Maryborough Lake Victoria (a small urban lake), NOT the Gippsland Lake Victoria. No cell count. End unknown.

GMW warning after high levels detected; Bendigo Advertiser published 11 Apr 2025. A VRFish/GMW notice later confirmed removal 'following a significant decline' but no firm removal date was captured, so end left null.
Closed to on-water recreation since 23 April 2025 due to blue-green algae (per 12 May 2025 article). No lift date found.
Warning confirmed ACTIVE as of 29 Apr 2025 (avoid direct contact). start_date is the report date, not confirmed onset. No numeric cell count. Which 'Green Lake' - GWMWater's Green Lake near Horsham.

Warning confirmed ACTIVE as of the article date 29 Apr 2025 (blue-green algae above safe recreation levels); actual issue date earlier and unknown, so 2025-04-29 is a latest-bound estimate, not the true onset. Distinct 2025 event separate from the 2020 Lonsdale warning.

Same 29 Apr 2025 article reports Taylors Lake warning had been LIFTED, implying an earlier 2025 warning that ended by 29 Apr 2025. No issue date and no exact lift date given; end_date set to the report date as an approximate upper bound. Low confidence - reconstructed from a 'lifted' mention only.
High levels confirmed as of 9 May 2025 (article 12 May 2025), alongside Lake Eppalock and Cairn Curran. Exact issue date not stated; start = confirmed high-level date. No lift date found.

RED ALERT (=warning) over a ~20km stretch Psyche Bend to Merbein plus Kings Billabong. Testing by Lower Murray Water; WaterNSW co-monitors the border river. Kings Billabong and Psyche Bend are Victorian waterbodies. start_date = published 6 Jul 2025; alert expected to persist at least 14 days. No numeric cell count published.

events.region: Hume Dam at Ebden, Upper Murray / VIC-NSW border
events.notes: Red alert issued Friday 22 Aug 2025 for Lake Hume at Ebden; lifted (returned to green/low) 8 Sep 2025 — 'red alert lifted more than two weeks after blue-green algae found'. Source: Border Mail 9047577 (pub 8 Sep 2025). A WaterNSW page exists (https://www.waternsw.com.au/water-services/water-quality/algae-alerts/algae-alerts/2025/blue-green-algae-red-alert-for-hume-dam-at-ebden) but returns HTTP 404 on fetch. No cell count stated.
Council notice dated Friday 7 November 2025: 'high levels of blue-green algae' detected at Kialla Lakes. No lift date or cells/mL figure given.
GMW warnings list (fetched 17 July 2026): warning issued 13 Nov 2025, still active as of 17 July 2026. Distinct from the Jan 2025 Hepburns Lagoon event.

Swan Hill Rural City Council release (published 19 November 2025) welcomes GMW lifting the Lake Boga BGA warning ahead of summer; Gannawarra Times reported the lift 20 Nov 2025. Original issue date not stated (2025). end_date approximated to 19 Nov 2025.
Council warning issued Friday 19 December 2025 after detecting high levels of blue-green algae at Lake Bartlett in Tatura; avoid direct contact. No cell count stated.
GMW notice dated 22 Jan 2026, high levels detected. Still listed as an active warning on the GMW alert page (last updated 17 Jul 2026), so end left null.

Star Mail (mailcommunity) report, 20 Feb 2026. Melbourne Water testing found toxic blue-green algae levels 'low' at Lillydale Lake at the time, not currently a severe health risk, but warned conditions can change rapidly; Yarra Ranges Council continues to advise against swimming. Classified 'watch' because levels were reported low (surveillance-level), not a full avoid-contact alert. start_date is the report date; actual detection date not stated. No cell counts given.

RED ALERT (=warning) issued 27 Feb 2026 by Lower Murray Water for high levels of potentially toxic blue-green algae; attributed to hot humid weather with levels fluctuating weekly. Corroborated by Sunraysia Daily (27 Feb 2026). No numeric cell count. Lift date not yet known as of research date.

GMW warnings list (fetched 17 July 2026): warning issued 23 March 2026, still active as of 17 July 2026. Corroborated by Shepparton News (24 March 2026); described as the 10th Eildon alert since 2004. Applies to all locations incl. Main Arm, Goughs Bay, Jamieson, Delatite, Bonnie Doon.
GMW notice dated Monday 30 Mar 2026, avoid contact after high levels detected. Still listed as active on GMW alert page (17 Jul 2026), so end left null.

Blue-green algae bloom warning at the Wilson Botanic Park lake; council advised avoiding contact and keeping pets away. Warning reported issued Monday 4 May 2026 (Berwick/Cranbourne Star News, published 8-9 May 2026). Council began treatment with beneficial bacteria expected to take ~3 weeks, so likely active until ~late May 2026. No end/lift date confirmed; no cell counts stated. Bloom attributed to warm weather, sunlight, low water movement and elevated nutrients.

Council media release published 13 May 2026: do not swim / avoid direct contact with blue-green algae affected water at Lake Victoria, Maryborough. Distinct from the 4 Apr 2025 warning. No cell count. End unknown.

undated_events.notes: EPA Victoria Facebook post titled 'Avoid contact with water in Edwardes Lake Park, Reservoir' (blue-green algae follow-up). Confirms a real avoid-contact warning event at Edwardes Lake, but the post could NOT be fetched (Facebook blocked by robots.txt), so the exact date is unconfirmed - only the search-result title was seen. Facebook post ID (~994977xxx) is close in magnitude to a Hume post from 2026, suggesting a likely 2026 event, but this is not confirmable. start_date left null; confidence low. Darebin's own current-status page (darebin.vic.gov.au Algal-blooms-at-Edwardes-Lake) showed 'no toxic blue-green algae' at time of check with no historical dates.
